engineIntelligence.h2: Porsche Taycan Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main electric drive units and battery systems fitted to the Porsche Taycan in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt drive unit costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Porsche Taycan EBG drive unit - electric rear motor replacement UK
knownFailures: Bearing wear - rear motor bearing degradation reported on early 2020-2021 units at higher mileage.
Thermal management - coolant circuit leaks affecting drive unit cooling on pre-facelift models.
cards.specs: Electric · Rear Drive Unit · 530hp · Permanent Magnet Synchronous
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt drive unit: £5,000-£15,000 (supply only)

cards.altText: Porsche Taycan Performance Battery Plus - 93kWh HV battery replacement UK
knownFailures: Cell degradation - gradual capacity loss over time; individual module failures possible on high-mileage units.
Thermal interface wear - cooling plate contact degradation affecting battery temperature management.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK battery exchange: £30,000-£48,000 (supply only, core return reduces cost by ~£6,000)

cards.altText: Porsche Taycan PAD-901-141-X drive unit - OEM rear motor replacement UK
knownFailures: Software calibration - early firmware versions requiring updates for optimal performance and efficiency.
Inverter component wear - power electronics degradation on high-load usage patterns.
cards.specs: Electric · Rear Drive Unit · 530-680hp · OEM Porsche Specification
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt OEM unit: £6,000-£16,000 (supply only)

commonProblems.h2: Porsche Taycan Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Porsche Taycan's electric powertrain is generally robust, but high-voltage components like the EBG rear drive unit and Performance Battery Plus packs can develop issues. Every rebuilt drive unit supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address original failure points - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. This section helps you decide between repair and replacement.

problems.h4: Taycan Drive Unit Bearing Failure - Why Early EBG Units Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Porsche Taycan 4S, Cross Turismo 4, base RWD (2020-2021, EBG drive unit)
problems.rootCause: Rear motor bearing wear accelerated by thermal cycling and high-load driving patterns on early production units.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Disassemble drive unit, replace bearings, reseal, software recalibration
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early before rotor damage; not suitable for high-mileage units

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit complete rebuilt drive unit with upgraded bearings, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for units with rotor damage or over 70k miles; restores full performance

problems.recommendation: On a Taycan 4S worth £40,000-£60,000, if bearing noise is detected early and the unit is under 60k miles, specialist bearing replacement may offer good value. For higher mileage or confirmed rotor damage, a rebuilt EBG unit is the more reliable long-term solution.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EBG drive unit prices for your Porsche Taycan

problems.h4: Taycan Battery Degradation - Range Loss & Cell Failure Patterns
problems.affectedModels: Porsche Taycan 4S PB+, Turbo, Turbo S, GTS (2020-2023, 93kWh Performance Battery Plus)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 80,000-120,000 miles (gradual degradation); cell failure can occur earlier
problems.rootCause: Lithium-ion cell chemistry degradation over charge cycles; individual module failures possible due to thermal stress or manufacturing variance.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnose faulty module(s), replace individual battery modules, rebalance pack, software update
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if degradation is isolated to 1-2 modules; requires specialist HV certification

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit complete rebuilt/exchange battery pack with core return, full warranty, thermal system refresh
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for widespread degradation or multiple module failures; restores original range

problems.recommendation: Battery degradation is gradual - if range loss is under 15% and no fault codes are present, monitoring may suffice. For confirmed module failures or >20% capacity loss, a battery exchange with core return offers the best value, reducing cost by ~£6,000 versus new.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Taycan Performance Battery Plus replacement

problems.h4: Taycan Onboard Charger Failure - AC Charging Issues
problems.affectedModels: Porsche Taycan all variants (2020-2023, 11kW AC onboard charger)
problems.rootCause: Power electronics component failure within the onboard charger unit, often triggered by voltage spikes or thermal stress.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty onboard charger module, software coding, HV system safety check
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for isolated charger failure; does not address drive unit or battery issues

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Comprehensive HV system check, replace charger, verify thermal management, update firmware
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if charging errors persist or multiple HV components show wear

problems.recommendation: Onboard charger failure is often misdiagnosed as a drive unit issue. If your Taycan won't AC charge but DC fast charging works, start with a specialist charger diagnostic before considering major component replacement.
problems.cta: Compare Taycan onboard charger replacement prices UK

problems.h4: Taycan Thermal Management System Failure - Cooling Circuit Issues
problems.affectedModels: Porsche Taycan all variants (2020-2023, pre-facelift thermal system)
problems.rootCause: Coolant hose degradation, pump failure, or sensor faults affecting battery and drive unit temperature regulation.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty pump, hose, or sensor; refill with EV-specific coolant; system bleed
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for isolated component failure; prevents escalation to drive unit damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace multiple thermal components, flush system, update thermal management software
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for recurring faults or high-mileage vehicles with multiple cooling issues

problems.recommendation: Thermal management faults often trigger warning lights before catastrophic failure. Address coolant leaks or pump noises promptly - a £1,000 repair today can prevent a £15,000 drive unit replacement tomorrow.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Taycan thermal system repair UK

problems.group: Don't Let Drive Unit Failure Write Off Your Porsche Taycan

Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Porsche Taycan and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Porsche Taycan Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every electric drive unit and battery capacity fitted to the Porsche Taycan is listed, with compatible engine codes, years and common failure notes.

items.title: Porsche Taycan EBG Drive Unit Electric Engines
items.description: The EBG rear drive unit powered the Taycan 4S and Cross Turismo variants from launch, delivering 530hp through Porsche's innovative two-speed transmission and 800V architecture for rapid charging and sustained performance.
commonFailurePoints: Rear motor bearing wear on early 2020-2021 units; coolant circuit leaks affecting thermal management
items.cta: Get quotes for Porsche Taycan EBG electric engines

items.title: Porsche Taycan PAD-901-141-X Performance Electric Engines
items.description: The PAD-901-141-X series represents Porsche's high-performance rear drive unit for Turbo and Turbo S variants, featuring enhanced cooling, locking differential capability, and software calibration for track-focused durability.
commonFailurePoints: Inverter component wear under sustained high-load usage; firmware calibration requirements on early production
items.cta: Compare Porsche Taycan PAD-901-141-X electric engine prices

items.title: Porsche Taycan Front Drive Unit Electric Engines
items.description: The auxiliary front drive unit (PAD-901-143) provides AWD capability and torque vectoring for 4S, Turbo and Cross Turismo variants, working in concert with the rear EBG unit for balanced performance.
commonFailurePoints: Less frequent than rear unit; occasional sensor or connector issues; thermal hose degradation on high-mileage examples
items.cta: Enquire about Porsche Taycan front drive unit electric engine availability

items.title: Porsche Taycan 79kWh Performance Battery Electric Engines
items.description: The base 79kWh Performance Battery was offered on early entry-level Taycan models, providing adequate range for urban use but less thermal headroom than the Plus variant for sustained high-performance driving.
commonFailurePoints: Faster thermal degradation under repeated fast charging; limited upgrade path versus PB+ variants
items.cta: Get quotes for Porsche Taycan 79kWh battery replacement

items.title: Porsche Taycan 93kWh Performance Battery Plus Electric Engines
items.description: The 93kWh Performance Battery Plus became the standard for most Taycan variants, offering improved range, better thermal management, and compatibility with higher power outputs across the model range.
commonFailurePoints: Gradual cell degradation over charge cycles; individual module failures possible on high-mileage units
items.cta: Compare Porsche Taycan 93kWh Performance Battery Plus prices

items.title: Porsche Taycan 105kWh Performance Battery Plus Electric Engines (Facelift)
items.description: The 105kWh Performance Battery Plus is standard on 2024+ facelift Taycan models, featuring improved energy density, faster charging capability, and enhanced thermal management for next-generation performance.
commonFailurePoints: Too recent for widespread failure data; early reports indicate improved reliability over 93kWh predecessor
items.cta: Enquire about Porsche Taycan 105kWh facelift battery availability

items.descriptor: The Porsche Taycan is exclusively battery-electric, with rear and front drive units paired with Performance Battery or Performance Battery Plus packs across all UK variants.
items.familiesLabel: Common Porsche Taycan Electric Drive Unit Families
families: EBG - primary rear drive unit for 4S/Cross Turismo, bearing wear risk on early units
PAD-901-141-X - high-performance rear unit for Turbo/Turbo S, inverter component considerations
PAD-901-143 - front auxiliary drive unit for AWD variants, less frequent failure profile

engineYears.h2: Porsche Taycan Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major drive unit and battery changes across the Taycan's production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their powertrain and compare rebuilt replacement prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The Taycan launched with EBG rear drive units and 93kWh Performance Battery Plus, establishing Porsche's 800V electric architecture.
keyChanges: First all-electric Porsche enters UK market with 4S, Turbo, Turbo S variants
EBG rear drive unit and PAD-901-141-X performance units introduced
Performance Battery (79kWh) and Performance Battery Plus (93kWh) options available
mainEngines: PAD-901-141-X - electric rear drive unit (Turbo/Turbo S, 680-762hp)
knownFor: Groundbreaking 800V charging capability for ultra-fast DC replenishment
Early bearing wear reports on EBG units after 50k+ miles
High replacement cost but strong performance retention with proper care
years.cta: Get quotes for 2020 Porsche Taycan replacement drive units
years.ticker: Taycan 4S rear motor noise • Turbo drive unit replacement cost • PB+ battery exchange price

years.description: Taycan range expands with Cross Turismo estate variants and GTS performance trim, introducing revised drive unit firmware and thermal management updates.
keyChanges: Cross Turismo estate body style launched with 4 and 4S variants
Taycan GTS introduced as mid-range performance option with 590hp
Revised thermal management software and bearing specifications for EBG units
mainEngines: EBG (revised spec) - electric rear drive unit (4S, GTS, Cross Turismo)
PAD-901-141-X (updated firmware) - electric rear drive unit (Turbo variants)
PAD-901-143 - front auxiliary drive unit (all AWD variants)
knownFor: Improved bearing durability on mid-production EBG units
Cross Turismo's raised ride height popular for UK road conditions
GTS offers strong performance/value balance for enthusiast owners
years.cta: Compare 2021-2022 Porsche Taycan drive unit prices UK
years.ticker: Cross Turismo drive unit compatibility • GTS motor replacement price • AWD front unit availability

years.description: Final pre-facelift year sees drive unit supersession updates (9J1-901-141-CX) and refined battery management software for improved longevity.
keyChanges: Final revision of pre-facelift drive units: 9J1-901-141-CX specification
Updated battery management software for improved cell balancing and thermal control
Last production year for 93kWh PB+ as standard before facelift transition
mainEngines: EBG (9J1-901-141-CX) - final pre-facelift rear drive unit revision
PAD-901-141-X (CX spec) - updated performance rear unit
knownFor: Most reliable pre-facelift specification with accumulated engineering refinements
Strong residual values for late-production examples
Ideal candidate for rebuilt drive unit upgrades with latest firmware
years.cta: Get quotes for 2023 Porsche Taycan replacement engines

years.description: Facelift Taycan introduces 105kWh PB+ as standard, updated Gen2 drive units, and enhanced charging performance across the range.
keyChanges: 105kWh Performance Battery Plus becomes standard across all variants
Gen2 EBG and PAD drive units with improved efficiency and thermal management
Revised exterior styling and interior technology; enhanced DC charging capability
mainEngines: Gen2 EBG - updated electric rear drive unit (4S, GTS, Cross Turismo)
Gen2 PAD-901-141-X - updated performance rear unit (Turbo variants)
PB+ 105kWh - new standard high voltage battery pack
popularModels: Porsche Taycan Turbo S Cross Turismo (2024+)
knownFor: Too recent for widespread failure data; early reports indicate improved reliability
Higher initial cost but improved long-term ownership economics
Backward compatibility considerations for replacement drive units
years.cta: Enquire about 2024+ Porsche Taycan facelift engine availability
years.ticker: Facelift drive unit compatibility • 105kWh battery exchange • Gen2 motor pricing

faq.h2: Porsche Taycan Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Porsche Taycan drive unit replacement cost?
items.answer: A rebuilt Porsche Taycan rear drive unit typically costs £5,000-£15,000 supply-only, while battery pack replacement ranges from £30,000-£48,000. Core return on batteries can reduce cost by ~£6,000.
keyPoints: EBG rear drive units: £5,000-£15,000 rebuilt supply-only
Performance Battery Plus exchange: £30,000-£48,000 with core return
Front drive units (AWD variants): £4,500-£12,000 typical range
Supply & fit labour adds £1,500-£3,500 depending on workshop
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Taycan drive unit prices for your variant

items.question: What causes Porsche Taycan rear motor bearing failure?
items.answer: Early EBG drive units (2020-2021) can develop bearing wear due to thermal cycling and high-load driving, typically appearing after 50,000-80,000 miles with whining noise from the rear axle.
keyPoints: Bearing wear most common on early-production EBG units
Symptoms include rear whining noise, especially under acceleration
Caught early, bearing replacement may suffice; advanced wear requires full unit rebuild
Revised bearing specifications introduced on 2022+ production units
items.cta: Get quotes for EBG bearing repair or replacement

items.question: Can a Porsche Taycan battery be repaired or must it be replaced?
items.answer: Individual battery modules can sometimes be replaced for isolated faults, but widespread degradation or multiple module failures typically require full battery pack exchange via the core return programme.
keyPoints: Module-level repair viable for single-cell or isolated module faults
Full pack exchange recommended for >20% capacity loss or multiple failures
Core return reduces battery replacement cost by approximately £6,000
All battery work requires HV-certified specialist workshop and Porsche diagnostics
items.cta: Enquire about Taycan battery module repair versus full exchange

items.question: Which Porsche Taycan variant has the most reliable drive unit?
items.answer: Late-production (2022-2023) Taycan 4S and GTS models with revised EBG specifications show the best reliability balance, benefiting from accumulated engineering refinements before the facelift.
keyPoints: 2022-2023 EBG units feature improved bearing and thermal specifications
GTS variant offers strong performance without Turbo-level thermal stress
Facelift (2024+) Gen2 units expected to show further reliability improvements
Regular thermal system maintenance critical for all variants' longevity
items.cta: Compare reliability data and prices for Taycan variants

items.question: How long does a Porsche Taycan drive unit last?
items.answer: With proper maintenance, Taycan drive units can exceed 150,000 miles, though bearing wear on early units may require attention around 60,000-80,000 miles for optimal longevity.
keyPoints: Well-maintained EBG units regularly exceed 150k miles in fleet use
Thermal management system health critical for drive unit lifespan
Software updates and coolant service intervals impact long-term reliability
Rebuilt units with upgraded components can restore like-new performance
items.cta: Find rebuilt Taycan drive units with extended warranty coverage

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Taycan drive unit versus selling the car?
items.answer: For Taycans valued over £35,000, a £5,000-£15,000 rebuilt drive unit typically offers better value than selling at a fault-affected price, especially with a 12-month warranty included.
keyPoints: Compare repair cost against fault-affected sale price differential
Rebuilt units include warranty, protecting your investment post-repair
High residual values of Taycans support repair over replacement decision
EngineMarket comparison helps identify best-value specialist for your variant
items.cta: Calculate repair vs sell value for your Porsche Taycan

items.question: What's the difference between 93kWh and 105kWh Taycan batteries?
items.answer: The 105kWh Performance Battery Plus (facelift) offers ~13% more capacity, improved thermal management, and faster charging versus the 93kWh PB+ found in pre-facelift Taycan models.
keyPoints: 105kWh provides approximately 30-40 miles additional real-world range
Enhanced thermal system supports sustained high-performance driving
Not interchangeable between pre-facelift and facelift chassis without modification
Core return exchange pricing applies to both capacities
items.cta: Compare 93kWh vs 105kWh Taycan battery replacement costs

items.question: Do Porsche Taycan front drive units fail as often as rear units?
items.answer: Front drive units (PAD-901-143) on AWD Taycans fail less frequently than rear EBG units, with most issues relating to sensors or connectors rather than core motor components.
keyPoints: Front units experience lower thermal and torque loads than rear counterparts
Common issues: connector corrosion, sensor faults, coolant hose wear
Replacement cost typically £4,500-£12,000 versus £5,000-£15,000 for rear units
AWD system diagnostics essential before condemning front unit
items.cta: Get quotes for Taycan front drive unit replacement

items.question: Can I fit a Taycan drive unit myself or is specialist fitting required?
items.answer: Taycan drive unit replacement requires HV-certified specialists due to 800V high-voltage systems; DIY fitting is strongly discouraged and may void warranties or insurance.
keyPoints: 800V architecture requires specialised HV safety equipment and training
Porsche PIWIS diagnostic system needed for coding and adaptation post-fit
Supply & fit packages available through EngineMarket's vetted specialist network
Improper installation risks personal injury and component damage
items.cta: Find HV-certified Taycan supply & fit specialists near you

items.question: Does a rebuilt Taycan drive unit come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes - all rebuilt and reconditioned Taycan drive units supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists.
keyPoints: 12-month warranty standard across EngineMarket's vetted supplier network
Warranty covers parts and labour for specified failure modes
Confirm exact terms with chosen supplier before ordering
Core return documentation required for battery exchange warranty validation
items.cta: Compare warranty terms and prices for Taycan rebuilt engines
